verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But Daniel foretells the exact time.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But perhaps, being unable, even they, to fight continually against plain facts, they will, without denying what is written, maintain that they are looking for these things, and that the Word of God is not yet come.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For this it is on which they are for ever harping, not blushing to brazen it out in the face of plain facts.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But on this one point, above all, they shall be all the more refuted, not at our hands, but at those of the most wise Daniel, who marks both the actual date, and the divine sojourn of the Saviour, saying: “Seventy weeks are cut short upon thy people, and upon the holy city, for a full end to be made of sin, and for sins to be sealed up, and to blot out iniquities, and to make atonement for iniquities, and to bring everlasting righteousness, and to seal vision and prophet, and to anoint a Holy of Holies; and thou shalt know and understand from the going forth of the word to restore and to build Jerusalem unto Christ the Prince” 3.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Perhaps with regard to the other (prophecies) they may be able even to find excuses and to put off what is written to a future time.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But what can they say to this, or can they face it at all?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Where not only is the Christ referred to, but He that is to be anointed is declared to be not man simply, but Holy of Holies; and Jerusalem is to stand till His coming, and thenceforth, prophet and vision cease in Israel.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: David was anointed of old, and Solomon and Ezechias; but then, nevertheless, Jerusalem and the place stood, and prophets were prophesying: God and Asaph and Nathan; and, later, Esaias and Osee and Amos and others.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: And again, the actual men that were anointed were called holy, and not Holy of Holies.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But if they shield themselves with the captivity, and say that because of it Jerusalem was not, what can they say about the prophets too?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For in fact when first the people went down to Babylon, Daniel and Jeremy were there, and Ezechiel and Aggæus and Zachary were prophesying.
